I2C hardware abstraction layer functions for initializing, enabling, disabling, and managing transfers.

# Purpose
This C header file declares a set of functions for managing I2C (Inter-Integrated Circuit) bus operations at a hardware abstraction layer (HAL). The functions include [`i2c_hal_init`](<#i2c_hal_init>), [`i2c_hal_enable`](<#i2c_hal_enable>), and [`i2c_hal_disable`](<#i2c_hal_disable>), which initialize, enable, and disable the I2C bus, respectively. The function [`i2c_hal_is_busy`](<#i2c_hal_is_busy>) checks if the I2C bus is currently busy. Additionally, [`i2c_hal_abort_transfer`](<#i2c_hal_abort_transfer>), [`i2c_hal_init_transfer`](<#i2c_hal_init_transfer>), and [`i2c_hal_start_transfer`](<#i2c_hal_start_transfer>) manage the initiation, starting, and aborting of data transfers on the I2C bus. The file includes the `board/board.h` header, which suggests that it relies on board-specific configurations or definitions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `board/board.h`
- `stdbool.h`
